channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Nvidia revenue forecasts for first quarter exceed estimates
item.description: Nvidia forecast first-quarter revenue above market estimates on Wednesday, expecting robust demand for its leading AI chips to persist as businesses spend heavily to expand generative artificial intelligence infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Amazon’s AI-powered Alexa+ wants to be your best friend—for $19.99 per month
item.description: Amazon on Wednesday unveiled a generative-AI infused Alexa that it says will allow the popular voice assistant to have more personality, check a user’s tone and even plan romantic dates.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: 6 motivi per i quali un team IT potrebbe non riuscire a soddisfare le aspettative
item.description: C’è una miriade di motivi per i quali un Chief Information Officer può ritenere importante ottenere il massimo dei voti per il proprio lavoro.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Niantic unveils Quest app to explore 3D images from around the world
item.description: Meta Quest 3 users will now be able to explore detailed 3D scans of sculptures, rock formations, plant life, and other interesting objects from around the world.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Federal Employee Warns of Cybersecurity Risks After Being Fired
item.description: A federal employee who was recently fired as part of massive government-wide cuts has warned that sensitive financial and health data belonging to millions of veterans is at risk of being stolen or compromised.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Apple shareholders reject proposal to scrap company's diversity programs
item.description: Apple shareholders rebuffed an attempt to pressure the technology trendsetter into joining President Donald Trump's push to scrub corporate programs designed to diversify its workforce.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Amazon's new AI-powered Alexa promises to be your 'best friend in a digital world' for a monthly fee
item.description: Amazon on Wednesday unveiled a generative-AI infused Alexa that it says will allow the popular voice assistant to have more personality, check a user’s tone and even plan romantic dates.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero And Wyatte Grantham-Philips, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NordVPN Research Reveals Alarming Cybersecurity Statistics: Billions of Malware Incidents and Fake URLs - 2024
item.description: NordVPN research highlights billions of malware incidents in 2024, with almost 85,000 fake Google URLs identified. Phishing remains a significant problem, emphasizing the need for caution when dealing with unsolicited communications.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Alexa Plus subscription pricing confirmed – and it's good news for Prime members
item.description: Amazon has announced the pricing for its new Alexa Plus voice assistant –and it could make a Prime subscription a no-brainer.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Gabbard moves to fire 100+ intel officers over explicit chat messages
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, the director of national intelligence, has moved to fire more than 100 intelligence officers over explicit chats they allegedly sent around in an internal agency messaging board.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Not known for political coverage, Wired takes a leading role in tracking Elon Musk's team
item.description: The tech outlet Wired set up a new politics team in time for the presidential election — but its most impactful work is coming after the election is over.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sony unveils its first lens with a massive 800mm reach – and it could be a dream optic for wildlife photography
item.description: Double delight: Sony unveils the FE 400-800mm F6.3-8 G OSS and FE 16mm F1.8 G lenses for its full-frame mirrorless cameras.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tesla feels the wrath of anti-Elon Musk backlash
item.description: Musk has attacked leaders of California, a state that played a pivotal role in the carmaker surviving its tumultuous early years and becoming one of the world’s most valuable companies..
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Google Pixel 9a price details just leaked online – and there's good and bad news
item.description: If you're saving up to invest in a Google Pixel 9a later this year, these are the price leaks you need to know about.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Musk business entanglements loom over CFPB siege
item.description: Elon Musk’s plans to add payment services to X, his social media platform, are looming large over the Trump administration’s push to dismantle the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), The tech billionaire has long voiced his hopes of turning X into an “everything app” that would also functio
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Finally, Samsung's first PCIe 5.0 SSD that you can actually buy is here; after a nearly two-year wait, meet the 9100 Pro
item.description: It took almost two years, but Samsung’s first PCIe 5.0 consumer SSD, the 9100 Pro, has finally arrived.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: What gives IT leaders pause as they look to integrate agentic AI with legacy infrastructure
item.description: Agentic AI was the big breakthrough technology for gen AI last year, and this year, enterprises will deploy these systems at scale.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 11 rules for writing better code
item.description: You can’t develop software and manage software projects for 30 years and not learn a thing or two or twelve about writing good code. Here are a near-dozen nuggets of coding wisdom I picked up along the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Plug-and-play web development with Astro
item.description: Astro is a full-stack JavaScript meta-framework that orchestrates reactive view technologies like React and Svelte. While there are numerous meta-frameworks (examples include Next.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Riyadh Air and IBM forge AI-Driven partnership to revolutionize aviation
item.description: In a groundbreaking move, Riyadh Air has announced a strategic partnership with IBM to integrate AI across its operations, aiming to establish itself as the world’s first digital-native airline.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Consumers are connected more than ever before
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: Sowmyanarayan Sampath, Fast Company

item.title: Why Donald Trump and Elon Musk probably aren’t breaking up any time soon
item.description: On Monday morning, anonymous hackers played a video on screens throughout the Department of Housing and Urban Development HQ in Washington, D.C. The AI-generated video jankily portrayed President Trump kissing Elon Musk’s feet, with an overlaid caption reading: “Long live the real king.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Atlassian refocuses Trello on individual task management
item.description: Atlassian’s Trello project management tool has received a facelift with features designed to organize meetings and ideas in a cohesive manner.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Former VA cybersecurity official warns DOGE access threatens sensitive data
item.description: A former Department of Veterans Affairs cybersecurity official warned that the Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) access to the department’s information threatens sensitive data stored within the organization.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: New European AI weather model to improve forecasts
item.description: The European Center for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) has made its new AI model, the Artificial Intelligence Forecasting System (AIFS), available to everyone free of charge. Medium-range weather forecasts are those that predict weather conditions for three to 15 days into the future.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco, Nvidia expand AI partnership to include Silicon One technology
item.description: Cisco and Nvidia have expanded their collaboration to support enterprise AI implementations by tying together Cisco’s Silicon One technology and Nvidia’s Ethernet networking platform.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: IBM acquires DataStax to boost watsonx’s generative AI capabilities
item.description: IBM has announced its intent to acquire database and related services provider DataStax for an undisclosed sum in an effort to boost watsonx’s generative AI capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 21 staffers in office overtaken by DOGE resign
item.description: More than 20 staffers who worked in the federal department overtaken by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) resigned Tuesday, saying they won’t use their technical skills to “jeopardize” Americans’ data and “compromise” essential government systems.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FAA testing Musk's Starlink at 3 sites to fix 'reliability' issue
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said it is testing the use of Elon Musk’s Starlink internet platform to fix connectivity issues in U.S. airspace. The agency, on Monday, stated that some remote areas in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Connolly calls on OPM to reverse CIO classification change
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly, the ranking member of the Oversight and Accountability Committee, said the CIOs should remain career-reserve SES positions. The post Connolly calls on OPM to reverse CIO classification change first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Why Nvidia’s earnings are important to the entire U.S. stock market
item.description: Nvidia stock’s movement carries more weight on the S&P 500 and other indexes than every company except Apple.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: AWS exec highlights key skills for success in the evolving AI-driven job market
item.description: According to the World Economic Forum’s recent Future of Jobs Report, 39% of workers’ existing skillsets will become outdated by 2030 due to economic uncertainty, the rise of generative AI (genAI) and rapid technological change.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Alibaba to invest $52 billion in AI and cloud over three years
item.description: Alibaba is planning to invest 380 billion yuan ($52 billion) in artificial intelligence and cloud computing over the next three years, its biggest commitment yet to AI-driven infrastructure. That’s more than its total spending on AI and cloud computing over the past decade, it said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google rolls out free version of Gemini Code Assist for individuals
item.description: Google is rolling out a free version of Gemini Code Assist, its tool for code completion, code generation, and code chat, with the aim of helping individual developers improve their productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft gives Azure AI users a place to experiment with latest technologies
item.description: With the pace of innovation in AI accelerating, it’s important for developers to get early access to new tools and technologies. To that end, Microsoft has introduced a module inside its Azure AI Foundry service that aims to bring the latest innovations and research in AI to developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A smarter approach to training AI models
item.description: Artificial intelligence, which was thought to be handily dominated by American labs and researchers, was thrown for a loop with DeepSeek’s R1. And yet, R1 is still subject to many of the same pitfalls as other models—we must make radical advances to move beyond the current limitations of AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World
